page: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory workloads. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. In stress-ng testing, the instance achieved a single-core score of 1563.29 and a multi-core score of 47304.54. Memory bandwidth tests show a cached read speed of 2620.77 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 49.12 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance recorded a Geekbench multi-core score of 12291.0 and a software compilation score of 33293.0. Redis SET operations reached 39.58 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 27.07 GB/sec.

Given its compute-heavy profile and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c5.12xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as high-performance web serving, software compilation, and batch processing. It is also capable of handling moderate database operations and small-to-medium language model inference, processing 135M model prompts at 1585.28 tokens per second. However, its lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and its weak performance on large 70B LLM models makes it unsuitable for heavy AI generation tasks without GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 47304.54 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12291.0. It supports AVX-512 vector instructions, enhancing its capability for cryptographic and mathematical operations. This instance is designed for compute-bound workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations, though its lack of local storage and GPU acceleration limits its effectiveness for large-scale AI inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor, designed for high-compute workloads on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring forty-eight virtual cores and advanced vector extensions for intensive processing workloads.

96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
